weights.summary: Adjust each criterion above the chosen preset.
weights.presetIntro: Each slider starts from the <bold>{{preset}}</bold> preset. Moving a slider deviates from the preset ("modified" badge).
weights.effectExplanation: A slider's effect depends on your team's situation. <b1>Sub-roles</b1>, <b2>kitchen/floor</b2>, <b3>fill contractual hours</b3>, <b4>moderate/heavy overtime</b4>, <b5>preferences</b5> and <b6>stability</b6> have a measurable effect in most cases. The criteria <b7>light overtime</b7>, <b8>concentrating overtime on volunteers</b8>, <b9>priorities</b9> and <b10>versatility</b10> only have a visible effect if your team has clearly differentiated employees (overtime volunteers, P1/P2/P3 tiers, contrasting availability).
weights.compareIntro_one: Simulates a {{count}}-week schedule for each configuration. Compliance rules (overtime, rest, rolling averages) need several weeks to stabilize.
weights.compareIntro_other: Simulates a {{count}}-week schedule for each configuration. Compliance rules (overtime, rest, rolling averages) need several weeks to stabilize.

reminders.label: Reminders
reminders.description: WhatsApp reminders sent to employees based on their published schedule.
reminders.dailyExplanation: reminder sent the evening before for next-day services.
reminders.weeklyExplanation: summary of the week's services sent on Sunday evening.

billing.portalError: Could not open the billing portal
billing.title: Subscription
billing.trialEnds: Free trial until {{date}} - no charge before this date
billing.pastDueWarning: Payment pending - please update your payment method.
billing.cancelScheduled: Cancellation scheduled for {{date}} - access kept until then.
billing.resubscribeError: Could not reactivate subscription. Please retry.

workerShares.title: Shared team
workerShares.intro: Sharing only adds a scheduling identity. HR, payroll, documents and notes stay attached to their restaurant.
workerShares.policyPlanningBody: The employee accepts the invitation before appearing in the target restaurant.
workerShares.policyPayrollBody: Files, notes, contracts and payroll exports stay separate by restaurant.
workerShares.policyHoursBody: Conflicts and caps are checked across all account restaurants; exports stay per restaurant.
workerShares.needSecondRestaurant: Add a second restaurant to the account to propose shared employees.
workerShares.globalEmpty: No active or pending authorization on the account.
workerShares.empty: No shared employee for this restaurant.

errors.restaurant_not_found: One of the restaurants is no longer accessible.
errors.invalid_worker_share_payload: Complete the restaurant, employee and role.
errors.owner_mismatch: Sharing is limited to restaurants in the same account.
errors.owner_manager_required: Only account managers can manage the shared team.
errors.inviter_not_allowed: Only account managers can invite a shared employee.
errors.revoker_not_allowed: Only account managers can remove a share.
errors.source_membership_required: This employee is no longer active in the source restaurant for this role.
errors.target_membership_exists: This employee already belongs to the target restaurant.
errors.authorization_not_pending: This invitation is no longer pending.

profile.title: Profile
profile.botLocaleHint: Bot currently French-only — other languages coming soon.
profile.includeSilaeDesc: Adds a flat Silae CSV to the monthly recap email, alongside the accounting payroll CSV.
profile.includeSilaeWarning: If payroll IDs are missing, the email still sends with a warning and without the Silae attachment.
profile.silaeCodesTitle: Silae codes for the active restaurant
profile.silaeCodesIntro: Codes used in the Silae export. They must match the target My Silae dossier setup.
profile.silaeCodesAnalyticHint: The restaurant is added automatically in the Analytical section column of the export.

autoStaffing.label: Pre-fill schedule in advance
autoStaffing.off: Auto-staffing disabled. Use the Auto button on the schedule to generate manually.
autoStaffing.on_one: Automatically fill the schedule {{count}} week ahead based on availability, priorities and templates.
autoStaffing.on_other: Automatically fill the schedule {{count}} weeks ahead based on availability, priorities and templates.
autoStaffing.publicationHint: Generation fills the draft. Publication happens from the schedule; HCR compliance checks the 15-day communication deadline.

weekPlanner.title: Targets schedule
weekPlanner.intro: Assign a staffing target to each week. Click to switch.

closures.title: Restaurant closures
closures.imposeLeaveTitle: Create approved leave for every active employee for this period
closures.imposeLeaveDescription: The <link>French Labour Code (L3141-13) ↗</link> allows employers to impose leave during a closure, with at least 30 days' notice. The reason "Imposed leave — closure" is automatically recorded.
closures.overlapError: A closure already exists for these dates
closures.shortNoticeConfirm: Only {{count}} day(s)' notice. French Labour Code L3141-13 requires at least 30 days to impose leave during a closure. Continue anyway?
closures.imposeFailed: Closure added, but imposing leaves failed.

services.title: Services
services.duplicateError: Each service must have a unique name

staffingTargets.title: Staffing targets
staffingTargets.introSingle: Number of employees per role per service.
staffingTargets.introMulti: Number of employees per role per service. Each target has its own services.
staffingTargets.selectTitulairesTitle: Pick the regulars for this target

overtime.title: Overtime
overtime.intro: Legal cap on overtime and distribution rule between employees. HCR convention: 39h/week threshold.
overtime.modeControlledDesc: Up to a weekly cap that you set
overtime.capExplanation39: No overtime - identical to strict mode.
overtime.capExplanation110: Up to {{count}}h of OT at 110% per employee per week.
overtime.capExplanation120: Up to 4h at 110% + {{count}}h at 120% per employee per week.
overtime.capExplanationMax: Up to 4h at 110% + 4h at 120% + 1h at 150% per employee per week. Legal maximum.
overtime.distributionIntro: When auto-staffing needs OT, who receives it first.
overtime.distributionWillingDesc: Volunteer employees receive OT first, then by priority
overtime.distributionPriorityDesc: P1 receives OT first, regardless of preferences
overtime.distributionEvenDesc: Spread OT evenly across eligible employees

optimizationStyle.title: Optimization style
optimizationStyle.intro: How the solver trades off contractual hours, stability and priorities. Affects auto-staffing and simulations.
optimizationStyle.equilibreDesc: Spreads effort fairly. Fills contractual hours before opening overtime.
optimizationStyle.equipeStableDesc: Favours the same employees on the same services from one week to the next.
optimizationStyle.economiqueDesc: Avoids overtime — the most direct lever on payroll cost.
optimizationStyle.resilienceDesc: Spreads hours evenly — nobody is overloaded and everyone can absorb the unexpected.

rules.title: Rules
rules.medicalDesc: Employees can flag medical leave. Medical requests are auto-approved - a medical certificate reminder appears.
rules.preferencesDesc: Allow employees to indicate when they prefer to work. Their preferences appear on their profile.

timeTracking.title: Time tracking
timeTracking.tapInOutOnDesc: Employees can clock in on arrival and clock out at the end.
timeTracking.tapInOutOffDesc: Only scheduled hours are counted on the hours pages.
timeTracking.adminConfirmDesc: On every clock event, WhatsApp asks the manager to confirm. The clock event is recorded immediately, but remains pending until confirmed by WhatsApp or from the Hours page.
timeTracking.syncDesc: Clocked hours replace scheduled hours in the monthly recap — payroll reflects the hours actually worked.
timeTracking.latenessDesc: The monthly recap stays based on the schedule. Lateness and early departures are simply listed on the employee record (and in the monthly email).
timeTracking.earlyTapDesc: If an employee clocks in before the scheduled time, the early minutes add to their paid hours. Otherwise only the clock-out matters — hours start at the scheduled time.

subRoles.title: Skills / Sub-roles
subRoles.intro: Set the level of detail of the positions for your team.
subRoles.explanation: <b1>Chef</b1> — leads the team and runs service. <b2>Sous-chef</b2> — automatically replaces the chef when absent (add to the list if needed). The other skills are cumulative.

hcrGrid.title: HCR grid — Gross hourly rates
hcrGrid.intro: HCR Collective Agreement (<link1>IDCC 1979 ↗</link1>), indicative 2026 schedule. Adjust the rates per the <link2>active amendment ↗</link2> or your company agreement.
hcrGrid.subRoleMappingIntro: Default assignment applied to new employees. Admins can override per-employee from the record.
hcrGrid.subRoleMappingEmpty: Enable sub-roles in the Rules tab to configure the mapping.
hcrGrid.contractDefaultsIntro: Pre-filled values when creating a new employee. <link1>CDI ↗</link1> · <link2>CDD ↗</link2> · <link3>Seasonal ↗</link3>

compliance.title: Compliance - Labour law
compliance.intro: HCR Collective Agreement (Hôtels, Cafés, Restaurants). Disabled rules are no longer enforced by the auto-planner and no longer raise alerts — the resulting schedule may be non-compliant on those points.

colors.title: Colour palette
colors.intro: Pick a colour palette for the schedule cards. Kitchen and floor each have their own colour family.

help.title: Contact support
help.intro: Describe your request or report an incident. Our team replies within 24 working hours.
help.details: Requests that can be handled in writing receive a direct email reply. When a phone call is needed, your IDs let us analyse your situation in advance; we then propose a time slot.

cronRuns.heading: Scheduled tasks
cronRuns.intro: Last run of each scheduled task. Failures are retried automatically.

emailRecipients.heading: Additional email recipients
emailRecipients.intro: Accountant, administrator, co-manager… Pick which emails each contact receives.
